review_cycle.overview: Specification for the implement-review-verify cycle that the architect manages for each implementation unit during Phase 2.

step.name: implement
step.success: Implementer returns attempt_completion with code-change summary.
step.failure: Implementer returns blocker — mark task blocked, escalate to coordinator.

step.name: code_review
step.action: Dispatch sdlc-code-reviewer with staging path and implementer's summary.
step.failure: Re-dispatch implementer with review feedback. Track iteration count in staging document.

step.name: qa_verification
step.action: Dispatch sdlc-qa with acceptance criteria and verification commands.
step.success: Mark task done in staging. Proceed to next task.
step.failure: Re-dispatch implementer with QA failure evidence. After implementer fix, restart from code_review step.

iteration_limits.limit: After 3 review rejections for the same task: - Mark task as blocked in staging with review history. - Return to coordinator with blocker details and all 3 review verdicts. - Do NOT continue dispatching.
After 2 QA failures for the same task: - Mark task as blocked in staging with QA failure evidence. - Return to coordinator with blocker details.

status_tracking.description: After each dispatch cycle, update the task status in the staging document. Status values: pending | in-progress | done | blocked.

transition.trigger: Review limit or QA limit reached.

final_issue_review.description: After all individual tasks are done, run a final full-issue review cycle.

steps.step: Dispatch sdlc-code-reviewer with full issue scope and combined task summaries.
If Approved: dispatch sdlc-qa for full-issue verification.
If Changes Required: identify which task(s) need fixes, re-dispatch implementer for those specific tasks only.
If final QA passes: return to coordinator with full completion summary.
